title: Conjecture 3.5
statement_text: There is an integer $\ell$ such that if $G$ is a directed-triangle-free digraph with $\alpha(D) = \alpha$, then $\gamma(G) \leq \alpha^{\ell}$.
context_text: The same authors previously conjectured in [8] that the dichromatic number $\vec{\chi}(G)$ of a directed-triangle-free digraph can be bounded as a polynomial function of $\alpha(G)$. Because $\gamma(G) \leq \alpha(G) \cdot \vec{\chi}(G)$ (Observation 3.4), Conjecture 3.5 is presented as a necessary consequence: if the dichromatic-number conjecture holds, so must this domination bound.
notes: The hypothesis uses $\alpha(D) = \alpha$ in the PDF source, but the digraph is consistently called $G$ throughout the paper; this appears to be a typo for $\alpha(G) = \alpha$.
paper_title: Domination and fractional domination in digraphs
